author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 13, 14.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You who were present yesterday, bear in mind that we were a long while discoursing of the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, where He says, “I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life;” and if we wished to go on discoursing of that light, we might still speak a long time; for it would be impossible for us to expound the matter in brief.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, let us follow Christ, the light of the world, that we may not be walking in darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We must fear the darkness,—not the darkness of the eyes, but that of the moral character; and even if it be the darkness of the eyes, it is not of the outer, but of the inner eyes, of those by which we discern, not between white and black, but between right and wrong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When our Lord Jesus Christ had spoken these things, the Jews answered, “Thou bearest record of thyself; thy record is not true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before our Lord Jesus Christ came, He lighted and sent many prophetic lamps before Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of these was also John Baptist, to whom the great Light itself, which is the Lord Christ, gave a testimony such as was given to no other man; for He said, “Among them that are born of women, there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet this man, than whom none was greater among those born of women, said of the Lord Jesus Christ, “I indeed baptize you in water; but He that is coming is mightier than I, whose shoe I am not worthy to loose.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how the lamps submits itself to the Day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Himself bears witness that the same John was indeed a lamp: “He was,” saith He, “a burning and a shining lamp; and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the Jews said to the Lord, “Tell us by what authority thou doest these things,” He, knowing that they regarded John the Baptist as a great one, and that the same whom they regarded as a great one had borne witness to them concerning the Lord, answered them, “I also will ask you one thing; tell me, the baptism of John, whence is it? from heaven, or from men?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thrown into confusion, they considered among themselves that, if they said, “From men,” they might be stoned by the people, who believed John to be a prophet; if they said, “From heaven,” He might answer them, “He whom ye confess to have been a prophet from heaven bore testimony to me, and ye have heard from him by what authority I do these things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw, then, that whichever of these two answers they made, they would fall into the snare, and they said, “We do not know.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord answered them, “Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I tell you not what I know, because you will not confess what you know.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Most justly, certainly, were they repulsed, and they departed in confusion; and that was fulfilled which God the Father says by the prophet in the psalm, “I have prepared a lamp for my Christ” (the lamp was John); “His enemies I will clothe with confusion.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Christ, then, had the witness of prophets sent before Him, of the heralds that preceded the judge: He had witness from John; but He was Himself the greater witness which He bore to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those men with their feeble eyes sought lamps, because they were not able to bear the day; for that same Apostle John, whose Gospel we have in our hands, says in the beginning of his Gospel, concerning John the Baptist: “There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came for a witness, to bear witness of the light, that all men might believe through him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was not the light, but was sent to bear witness of the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That was the true light, that lighteth every man coming into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If “every man,” therefore also lighteth John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence also the same John says, “We all have received out of His fullness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore discern ye these things, that your minds may profit in the faith of Christ, that ye be not always babes seeking the breasts and shrinking from solid food.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You ought to be nourished and to be weaned by our holy mother the Church of Christ, and to come to more solid food by the mind, not by the belly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This discern ye then, that the light which enlighteneth is one thing, another that which is enlightened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For also our eyes are called lights; and every man thus swears, touching his eyes, by these lights of his: “So may my lights live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a customary oath.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let these lights, if lights they are, be opened, and shine for thee in thy closed chamber, when the light is not there; they certainly cannot.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, as these which we have in our face, and call lights, when they are both healthy and open, need the help of light from without,—which being removed or not brought in, though they are sound and are open, yet they do not see,—so our mind, which is the eye of the soul, unless it be irradiated by the light of truth, and wondrously shone upon by Him who enlightens and is not enlightened, will not be able to come to wisdom nor to righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to live righteously is for us the way itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how can he on whom the light does not shine but stumble in the way?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And hence, in such a way, we have need of seeing, in such a way it is a great thing to see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Tobias had the eyes in his face closed, and the son gave his hand to the father; and yet the father, by his instruction, pointed out the way to the son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews then answered, “Thou bearest witness of thyself; thy witness is not true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see what they hear; let us also hear, yet not as they did: they despising, we believing; they wishing to slay Christ, we desiring to live through Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let this difference distinguish our ears and minds from theirs, and let us hear what the Lord answers to the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered and said to them, Though I bear witness of myself, my witness is true; because I know whence I came and whither I go.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The light shows both other things and also itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou lightest a lamp, for instance, to look for thy coat, and the burning lamp affords thee light to find thy coat; dost thou light the lamp to see itself when it burns?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A burning lamp is indeed capable at the same time of exposing to view other things which the darkness covered, and also of showing itself to thine eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also the Lord Christ distinguished between His faithful ones and His Jewish enemies, as between light and darkness: as between those whom He illuminated with the ray of faith, and those on whose closed eyes He shed His light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, too, the sun shines on the face of the sighted and of the blind; both alike standing and facing the sun are shone upon in the flesh, but both are not enlightened in the eyesight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one sees, the other sees not: the sun is present to both, but one is absent from the present sun.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So likewise the Wisdom of God, the Word of God, the Lord Jesus Christ, is everywhere present, because the truth is everywhere, wisdom is everywhere.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One man in the east understands justice, another man in the west understands justice; is justice which the one understands a different thing from that which the other understands?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In body they are far apart, and yet they have the eyes of their minds on one object.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The justice which I, placed here, see, if justice it is, is the same which the just man, separated from me in the flesh by ever so many days’ journey, also sees, and is united to me in the light of that justice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the light bears witness to itself; it opens the sound eyes and is its own witness, that it may be known as the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how about the unbelievers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not present to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is present also to them, but they have not eyes of the heart with which to see it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the sentence fetched from the Gospel itself concerning them: “And the light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehended it not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence the Lord saith, and saith truly, “Though I bear witness of myself, my witness is true; because I know whence I came and whither I go.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He meant us to understand the Father here: the Son gave glory to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Himself the equal glorifies Him by whom He was sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How ought man to glorify Him by whom he was created!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I know whence I came and whither I go.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who speaks to you in person has what He has not left, and yet He came; for by coming He departed not thence, nor has He forsaken us by returning thither.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why marvel ye?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is God: this cannot be done by man; it cannot be done even by the sun.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When it goes to the west it leaves the east, and until it returns to the east, when about to rise, it is not in the east; but our Lord Jesus Christ both comes and is there, both returns and is here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the evangelist himself speaking in another place, and, if thou canst, understand it; if not, believe it: “God,” saith he, “no man hath ever seen, but the only-begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He hath declared Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not was in the bosom of the Father, as if by coming He had quitted the Father’s bosom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here He was speaking, and yet He declared that He was there; and when about to depart hence, what said He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The witness of the light then is true, whether it be manifesting itself or other things; for without light thou canst not see light, and without light thou canst not see any other thing whatever that is not light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If light is capable of showing other things which are not lights, is it not capable of showing itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not that discover itself, without which other things cannot be made manifest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A prophet spoke a truth; but whence had he it, unless he drew it from the fountain of truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John spoke a truth; but whence he spoke it, ask himself: “We all,” saith he, “have received of His fullness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore our Lord Jesus Christ is worthy to bear witness to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in any case, my brethren, let us who are in the night of this world hear also prophecy with earnest attention: for now our Lord willed to come in humility to our weakness and the deep night-darkness of our hearts: He came as a man to be despised and to be honored, He came to be denied and to be confessed; to be despised and to be denied by the Jews, to be honored and confessed by us: to be judged and to judge; to be judged unjustly, to judge righteously.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such then He came that He behoved to have a lamp to bear witness to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what need was there that John should, as a lamp, bear witness to the day, if the day itself could be looked upon by our weakness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we could not look upon it: He became weak for the weak; by infirmity He healed infirmity; by mortal flesh He took away the death of the flesh; of His own body He made a salve for our eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, the Lord is come, and since we are still in the night of the world, it behoves us to hear also prophecies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is from prophecy that we convince gainsaying pagans.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is Christ? says the pagan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom we reply, He whom the prophets foretold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What prophets? asks he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We quote Isaiah, Daniel, Jeremiah, and other holy prophets: we tell him that they came long before Christ, by what length of time they preceded His coming.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We make this reply then: Prophets came before Him, and they foretold His coming.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One of them answers: What prophets?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We quote for him those which are daily read to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, said he, Who are these prophets?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We answer: Those who also foretold the things which we see come to pass.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he urges: You have forged these for yourselves, you have seen them come to pass, and have written them in what books you pleased, as if their coming had been predicted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here in opposition to pagan enemies the witness of other enemies offers itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We produce books written by the Jews, and reply: Doubtless both you and they are enemies of our faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence are they scattered among the nations, that we may convince one class of enemies by another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the book of Isaiah be produced by the Jews, and let us see if it is not there we read, “He was led as a sheep to be slaughtered, and as a lamb before his shearer was dumb, so He opened not His mouth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In humility His judgment was taken away; by His bruises we are healed: all we as sheep went astray, and He was delivered up for our sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold one lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let another be produced, let the psalm be opened, and thence, too, let the foretold suffering of Christ be quoted: “They pierced my hands and my feet, they counted all my bones: but they considered me and gazed upon me, they parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture they cast the lot.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My praise is with Thee; in the great assembly will I confess to Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All the ends of the earth shall be reminded, and be converted to the Lord: all countries of the nations shall worship in His sight; for the kingdom is the Lord’s, and He shall have dominion over the nations.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let one enemy blush, for it is another enemy that gives me the book.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But lo, out of the book produced by the one enemy, I have vanquished the other: nor let that same who produced me the book be left; let him produce that by which himself also may be vanquished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I read another prophet, and I find the Lord speaking to the Jews: “I have no pleasure in you, saith the Lord, nor will I accept sacrifice at your hands: for from the rising of the sun even to his going down, a pure sacrifice is offered to my name.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou dost not come, O Jew, to a pure sacrifice; I prove thee impure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, even lamps bear witness to the day, because of our weakness, for we cannot bear and look at the brightness of the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In comparison, indeed, with unbelievers, we Christians are even now light; as the apostle says, “For ye were once darkness, but now light in the Lord: walk as children of light:” and he says elsewhere, “The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast away the works of darkness, and put on us the armor of light; let us walk honestly as in the day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet that even the day in which we now are is still night, in comparison with the light of that to which we are to come, listen to the Apostle Peter: he says that a voice came to the Lord Christ from the excellent glory, “Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This voice,” said he, “which came from heaven, we heard, when we were with Him in the holy mount.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because we were not there, and have not then heard this voice from heaven, the same Peter says to us, “And we have a more sure word of prophecy.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have not heard the voice come from heaven, but you have a more sure word of prophecy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord Jesus Christ, foreseeing that there would be certain wicked men who would calumniate His miracles, by attributing them to magical arts, sent prophets before Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, supposing He was a magician, and by magical arts caused that He should be worshipped after His death, was He then a magician before He was born?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the prophets, O man dead, and breeding the worms of calumny, hear the prophets: I read, hear them who came before the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We have,” saith the Apostle Peter, “a more sure word of prophecy, to which ye do well to give heed, as to a lamp in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day-star arise in your hearts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, our Lord Jesus Christ shall come, and, as the Apostle Paul also says, will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the thoughts of the heart, that every man may have praise from God; then, in presence of such a day, lamps will not be needed: no prophet shall then be read to us, no book of an apostle shall be opened; we shall not require the witness of John, we shall not need the Gospel itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly all Scriptures shall be taken out of the way,—which, in the night of this world, were as lamps kindled for us that we might not remain in darkness,—when all these are taken away, that they may not shine as if we needed them, and the men of God, by whom these were ministered to us, shall themselves, together with us, behold that true and clear light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, what shall we see after these aids have been removed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherewith shall our mind be fed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherewith shall our gaze be delighted?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence shall arise that joy which neither eye hath seen, nor ear heard, nor hath gone up into the heart of man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I beseech you, love with me, by believing run with me: let us long for our home above, let us pant for our home above, let us feel that we are strangers here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we see then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Gospel now tell us: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou shalt come to the fountain from which a little dew has already besprinkled thee: thou shalt see that very light, from which a ray was sent aslant and through many windings into thy dark heart, in its purity, for the seeing and bearing of which thou art being purified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John himself says, and this I cited yesterday: “Beloved, we are the sons of God; and it hath not yet appeared what we shall be: we know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him even as He is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I feel that your affections are being lifted up with me to the things that are above: but the body, which is corrupt, weighs down the soul; and, the earthly habitation depresses the mind while meditating many things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am about to lay aside this book, and you too are going to depart, every man to his own house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It has been good for us to have been in the common light, good to have been glad therein, good to have rejoiced therein; but when we part from one another, let us not depart from Him.
